perturbation.original_text: For purposes of this Agreement, "Confidential Information" shall include all information or material that has or could have commercial value or other utility in the business in which Disclosing Party is engaged. If Confidential Information is in written form, the Disclosing Party shall label or stamp the materials with the word "Confidential" or some similar warning. If Confidential Information is transmitted orally, the Disclosing Party shall promptly provide writing indicating that such oral communication constituted Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: For purposes of this Agreement, "Confidential Information" shall include all information or material that has or could have commercial value or other utility in the business in which Disclosing Party is engaged. If Confidential Information is in written form and the Disclosing Party desires to protect it, the Disclosing Party shall label or stamp the materials with the word "Confidential" or some similar warning. If Confidential Information is transmitted orally, the Disclosing Party may, at their discretion, provide writing indicating that such oral communication constituted Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that if Confidential Information is in written form, the Disclosing Party shall label it and if transmitted orally, the Disclosing Party shall promptly provide writing. The modified version replaces 'shall' with 'desires to protect' and 'may, at their discretion' respectively, weakening the obligation to protect Confidential Information. This contradicts the core purpose of the NDA, which is to ensure strict confidentiality. This is an in-text contradiction because the definition is intended to set the standard for how Confidential Information will be treated, and this change undermines that standard.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Receiving Party shall hold and maintain the Confidential Information in strictest confidence for the sole and exclusive benefit of the Disclosing Party. Receiving Party shall carefully restrict access to Confidential Information to employees, contractors and third parties as is reasonably required and shall require those persons to sign nondisclosure restrictions at least as protective as those in this Agreement. Receiving Party shall not, without the prior written approval of Disclosing Party, use for Receiving Party's benefit, publish, copy, or otherwise disclose to others, or permit the use by others for their benefit or to the detriment of Disclosing Party, any Confidential Information. Receiving Party shall return to Disclosing Party any and all records, notes, and other written, printed, or tangible materials in its possession pertaining to Confidential Information immediately if Disclosing Party requests it in writing.
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the obligation to protect Confidential Information by replacing 'shall' with 'desires to protect' and 'may, at their discretion'. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states 'Receiving Party shall hold and maintain the Confidential Information in strictest confidence', creating a strong conflict in the obligations of the receiving party, thus creating an in-text contradiction based on the structural flaws definition.

perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party's obligations under this Agreement do not extend to information that is: (a) publicly known at the time of disclosure or subsequently becomes publicly known through no fault of the Receiving Party; (b) discovered or created by the Receiving Party before disclosure by Disclosing Party; (c) learned by the Receiving Party through legitimate means other than from the Disclosing Party or Disclosing Party's representatives; or (d) is disclosed by Receiving Party with Disclosing Party's prior written approval.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party's obligations under this Agreement do not extend to information that is: (a) publicly known at the time of disclosure; (b) discovered or created by the Receiving Party; (c) learned by the Receiving Party; or (d) is disclosed by Receiving Party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clauses 'or subsequently becomes publicly known through no fault of the Receiving Party', 'before disclosure by Disclosing Party', 'through legitimate means other than from the Disclosing Party or Disclosing Party's representatives', and 'with Disclosing Party's prior written approval' the Receiving Party now has a much wider range of scenarios where they are not obligated to keep information confidential. This directly contradicts the overall purpose of the NDA to protect confidential information and creates an in-text contradiction with the obligations outlined elsewhere in the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Receiving Party shall hold and maintain the Confidential Information in strictest confidence for the sole and exclusive benefit of the Disclosing Party. Receiving Party shall carefully restrict access to Confidential Information to employees, contractors and third parties as is reasonably required and shall require those persons to sign nondisclosure restrictions at least as protective as those in this Agreement. Receiving Party shall not, without the prior written approval of Disclosing Party, use for Receiving Party's benefit, publish, copy, or otherwise disclose to others, or permit the use by others for their benefit or to the detriment of Disclosing Party, any Confidential Information. Receiving Party shall return to Disclosing Party any and all records, notes, and other written, printed, or tangible materials in its possession pertaining to Confidential Information immediately if Disclosing Party requests it in writing.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the scope of exceptions to the Receiving Party's confidentiality obligations by removing key qualifiers. For example, the original text excused information that 'subsequently becomes publicly known through no fault of the Receiving Party', while the changed text only excludes information 'publicly known at the time of disclosure'. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that the 'Receiving Party shall hold and maintain the Confidential Information in strictest confidence'. The changes provide loopholes where the Receiving Party could disclose information initially confidential if it later becomes public, or was discovered/learned, without violating the agreement, thus creating an in-text contradiction with the original NDA's intentions.

perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party shall carefully restrict access to Confidential Information to employees, contractors and third parties as is reasonably required and shall require those persons to sign nondisclosure restrictions at least as protective as those in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party shall restrict access to Confidential Information to employees, contractors and third parties. There is no obligation to have these individuals sign nondisclosure agreements.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates that the Receiving Party ensures that anyone with access to Confidential Information signs nondisclosure agreements. By removing 'carefully restrict access to Confidential Information to employees, contractors and third parties as is reasonably required and shall require those persons to sign nondisclosure restrictions at least as protective as those in this Agreement' and replace with 'Receiving Party shall restrict access to Confidential Information to employees, contractors and third parties. There is no obligation to have these individuals sign nondisclosure agreements.' This creates an in-text contradiction to the purpose of the NDA to prevent unauthorized disclosure, as Confidential Information can now be exposed without any safeguard, creating uncertainty about the obligations of the receiving party to maintain confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For purposes of this Agreement, "Confidential Information" shall include all information or material that has or could have commercial value or other utility in the business in which Disclosing Party is engaged. If Confidential Information is in written form, the Disclosing Party shall label or stamp the materials with the word "Confidential" or some similar warning. If Confidential Information is transmitted orally, the Disclosing Party shall promptly provide writing indicating that such oral communication constituted Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement for individuals accessing confidential information to sign nondisclosure agreements. This directly contradicts the purpose of an NDA, which is to protect confidential information. The contradicted text defines 'Confidential Information' and implies an expectation of protection, which is undermined by the change allowing access without safeguards. This creates a strong in-text contradiction as it makes the definition of confidentiality and its protection uncertain, leading to potential disputes and unenforceability.

perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party shall return to Disclosing Party any and all records, notes, and other written, printed, or tangible materials in its possession pertaining to Confidential Information immediately if Disclosing Party requests it in writing.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party shall return to Disclosing Party any records pertaining to Confidential Information if Disclosing Party requests it in writing. Notes and other tangible materials are not required to be returned.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'any and all records, notes, and other written, printed, or tangible materials' to 'any records pertaining to Confidential Information if Disclosing Party requests it in writing. Notes and other tangible materials are not required to be returned.', the Receiving Party is no longer obligated to return notes and other tangible materials. This creates an in-text contradiction because, without notes and other tangible materials, the Disclosing Party has less control over where Confidential Information might exist and creates uncertainty about the comprehensive retrieval of Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Receiving Party shall hold and maintain the Confidential Information in strictest confidence for the sole and exclusive benefit of the Disclosing Party. Receiving Party shall carefully restrict access to Confidential Information to employees, contractors and third parties as is reasonably required and shall require those persons to sign nondisclosure restrictions at least as protective as those in this Agreement. Receiving Party shall not, without the prior written approval of Disclosing Party, use for Receiving Party's benefit, publish, copy, or otherwise disclose to others, or permit the use by others for their benefit or to the detriment of Disclosing Party, any Confidential Information. Receiving Party shall return to Disclosing Party any and all records, notes, and other written, printed, or tangible materials in its possession pertaining to Confidential Information immediately if Disclosing Party requests it in writing.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original text by removing the requirement to return 'notes and other tangible materials'. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states 'Receiving Party shall return to Disclosing Party any and all records, notes, and other written, printed, or tangible materials'. By explicitly excluding notes and tangible materials from the return obligation, the changed text creates a direct inconsistency within the document, making the comprehensive retrieval of Confidential Information uncertain. This fits the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The nondisclosure provisions of this Agreement shall survive the termination of this Agreement and Receiving Party's duty to hold Confidential Information in confidence shall remain in effect until the Confidential Information no longer qualifies as a trade secret or until Disclosing Party sends Receiving Party written notice releasing Receiving Party from this Agreement, whichever occurs first.
perturbation.changed_text: The nondisclosure provisions of this Agreement shall terminate with this Agreement. There will be no residual duty on the Receiving Party to hold Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'The nondisclosure provisions of this Agreement shall survive the termination of this Agreement and Receiving Party's duty to hold Confidential Information in confidence shall remain in effect until the Confidential Information no longer qualifies as a trade secret or until Disclosing Party sends Receiving Party written notice releasing Receiving Party from this Agreement, whichever occurs first.' to 'The nondisclosure provisions of this Agreement shall terminate with this Agreement. There will be no residual duty on the Receiving Party to hold Confidential Information.' negates the long-term effect of the NDA. This creates an in-text contradiction because now, all previous sections detailing protection of information are negated. It undermines the core intent of the agreement to safeguard Confidential Information beyond the initial term of the Agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Receiving Party shall hold and maintain the Confidential Information in strictest confidence for the sole and exclusive benefit of the Disclosing Party. Receiving Party shall carefully restrict access to Confidential Information to employees, contractors and third parties as is reasonably required and shall require those persons to sign nondisclosure restrictions at least as protective as those in this Agreement. Receiving Party shall not, without the prior written approval of Disclosing Party, use for Receiving Party's benefit, publish, copy, or otherwise disclose to others, or permit the use by others for their benefit or to the detriment of Disclosing Party, any Confidential Information. Receiving Party shall return to Disclosing Party any and all records, notes, and other written, printed, or tangible materials in its possession pertaining to Confidential Information immediately if Disclosing Party requests it in writing.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that the nondisclosure provisions terminate with the agreement and there is no residual duty on the Receiving Party. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which mandates the Receiving Party to maintain Confidential Information in the strictest confidence, restrict access, and prohibits unauthorized use or disclosure indefinitely. The changed text eliminates these obligations, creating a clear and irreconcilable conflict with the previously stated duty to protect Confidential Information, undermining the intent of the agreement to safeguard Confidential Information.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement expresses the complete understanding of the parties with respect to the subject matter and supersedes all prior proposals, agreements, representations, and understandings. This Agreement may not be amended except in writing signed by both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement expresses the complete understanding of the parties with respect to the subject matter and supersedes all prior proposals, agreements, representations, and understandings. This Agreement may be amended orally by either party at any time.
perturbation.explanation: The original states that 'This Agreement may not be amended except in writing signed by both parties.' The changed text states 'This Agreement may be amended orally by either party at any time.' This creates a direct in-text contradiction because it changes the requirement of formal written modifications requiring signatures to allow oral amendments by either party. This introduces ambiguity and makes the contract's terms uncertain.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement expresses the complete understanding of the parties with respect to the subject matter and supersedes all prior proposals, agreements, representations, and understandings. This Agreement may not be amended except in writing signed by both parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that amendments must be in writing and signed by both parties. The changed text directly contradicts this by stating that amendments can be made orally by either party at any time. The contradicted text is the same as the original text, so the changed text creates a direct and irreconcilable conflict regarding the acceptable method for amending the agreement, meeting the definition of a Structural Flaw - In Text Contradiction, as a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.
